thinking: I face a mostly empty cafe room with a yellow rectangular table and chairs on the left, where one female customer sits quietly near the far side of the table; a kitchenette with sink, cabinets, stove, pots, kettle, and refrigerator is visible behind her. I slowly move and pan leftward around the table, bringing the seated customer and kitchen area more fully into view while the customer remains seated and looks generally toward me. Midway through, the customer raises one hand high above shoulder level, appearing to signal for attention or service, then keeps watching me as I continue approaching. I end close to the table edge, angled down toward the tabletop and the customer, with my hand briefly appearing near the table as if preparing to engage or serve her.
caption: CLERK: Intended to approach and attend to the seated customer, and did so by walking/panning closer to her table and orienting toward her. CUSTOMER: Intended to get assistance or place a request, and expressed it by staying seated, looking at the clerk, and raising her hand to signal.
